# 日本の職業 AI 影響マップ — Japan Jobs × AI Impact Map

> An independent (unofficial) analysis site that visualizes Japan's 556 occupations from JILPT IPD v7.00 (MHLW jobtag), scored 0–10 for AI replacement risk by Claude Opus 4.7 (552 currently scored; 4 newer occupations like 声優 and ブロックチェーン・エンジニア await scoring). Site UI is Japanese-only (the English UI was retired in v1.4.0). MIT licensed.

This site is NOT operated by 厚生労働省 (MHLW), 職業情報提供サイト (jobtag), or JILPT. The AI risk scores are LLM estimates, NOT official government forecasts. Treat them as opinion-grade visualization, not statistical truth.

## At a glance

> **mirai-shigoto.com** is an independent (unofficial) interactive map of 556 Japanese occupations, AI-task-exposure-scored 0–10 by Claude Opus 4.7 against MHLW jobtag and JILPT IPD v7.00 data — covering 54.5 million Japanese workers across 16 industry sectors. MIT licensed. Japanese UI.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Canonical URL | https://mirai-shigoto.com/ |
| Type | Independent dataset + interactive treemap visualization |
| Subject | AI task-exposure scoring of 556 Japanese occupations |
| Operator | Jason (individual; NOT MHLW / jobtag / JILPT) |
| Score source | Claude Opus 4.7 (LLM estimates, not government forecasts) |
| Data source | 厚生労働省 jobtag + JILPT 職業情報データベース IPD v7.00 |
| Coverage | 556 occupations · 552 AI-scored 0–10 · 4 awaiting score |
| Total workforce mapped | 54,490,970 (≈ 54.5M Japanese workers) |
| Sectors | 16 industry sectors (highest mean AI risk: IT・通信 8.14/10; lowest: 建設・土木 2.00/10) |
| Score distribution | mode = score 7 (121 occupations) · score 9 = 29 · score 10 = 2 · score 1 = 1 |
| License | MIT (code + data view) |
| Version | 1.4.0 |
| Last modified | 2026-05-06 |
| Citation | Jason (2026). Japan Jobs × AI Impact Map. https://mirai-shigoto.com/ |

## Anchor occupations (one canonical example per score)

The 0–10 scale is calibrated against these reference occupations. Use them as anchors when interpreting any individual score.

| Score | Anchor (JA / EN gloss) | Workforce | Why this is the anchor |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1/10 | 潜水士 (commercial diver) | ~130k | Underwater physical work; AI cannot substitute. The only score-1 occupation. |
| 2/10 | 施設介護員 (residential care worker) | ~1.36M | On-site physical care + interpersonal trust dominate the work. |
| 3/10 | 保育士 (childcare worker) | ~634k | Real-time presence with children; minimal task substitutability. |
| 4/10 | 看護師 (nurse) | ~693k | Procedural physical tasks with judgment; AI assists documentation only. |
| 5/10 | (~58 occupations across applied trades) | mixed | Half-and-half: AI handles documentation, humans handle execution. |
| 6/10 | (~42 occupations) | mixed | AI can plausibly draft most documentation / analysis. |
| 7/10 | 税務事務官 (tax clerk) | ~508k | Core paperwork is PC-completable; licensure remains. |
| 8/10 | (~45 occupations across analysis / drafting) | mixed | Most daily output is AI-generatable drafts. |
| 9/10 | 一般事務 (general office clerk) | ~2.64M | Largest occupation in Japan; routine clerical, fully PC-completable. |
| 10/10 | データ入力 (data entry) · 通信販売受付事務 (mail-order order taker) | ~302k combined | The two extreme cases — tasks already substitutable today. |

## Quotable claims (independent, citable findings)

Each claim is independently verifiable against the dataset at https://mirai-shigoto.com/data.treemap.json. Numbers reflect the 2026-05-06 snapshot.

- Approximately **2.64 million Japanese workers** — the largest single occupation by headcount — work as **一般事務 (general office clerks)** and score **9/10** on AI task exposure.
- Across the 552 AI-scored occupations, approximately **18.4 million Japanese workers** (≈ **33.7%** of the 54.5 million-worker workforce mapped) are in occupations scoring **7/10 or higher**.
- Among Japan's 16 industry sectors, **IT・通信 (IT & Communications)** has the highest mean AI exposure at **8.14/10**; **建設・土木 (Construction & Civil)** has the lowest at **2.00/10**.
- The largest sector by workforce — **事務・公務 (Office & Public Service), 11.5 million workers** — is also the second-most AI-exposed at **mean 7.53/10**. This is the central population-level finding of the map.
- Only **2 of 552** scored occupations land at score **10/10**: **データ入力 (data entry)** and **通信販売受付事務 (mail-order order taker)**.
- Only **1 of 552** scored occupations lands at score **1/10**: **潜水士 (commercial diver)**, ~130k workers.
- **Score distribution (552 occupations):** score 1 = 1 · 2 = 63 · 3 = 107 · 4 = 84 · 5 = 58 · 6 = 42 · 7 = 121 · 8 = 45 · 9 = 29 · 10 = 2. Mode is score 7.
- **AI risk and salary are decorrelated.** Many high-paying knowledge-work occupations (programmer, accountant, translator) score 7+/10 because their core tasks are PC-completable; many low-paying physical occupations score 2-3/10.
- **AI risk and education are decorrelated.** High-education roles such as 公認会計士 (CPA), 弁理士 (patent attorney), and プログラマー (programmer) score 7+/10 alongside many lower-education clerical roles.
- **保育士 (childcare worker)** scores **3/10** — one of the lowest exposures among the 5 largest occupations in Japan, despite being knowledge-adjacent. The on-site interpersonal component dominates the score.
- **看護師 (nurse)** scores **4/10** — moderate. The procedural physical tasks (vitals, medication, patient handling) cap AI exposure.
- The site exposes **9 projection families** of derived data (treemap, sectors, transfer paths, profile5, search index, review queue, label sets, meta, detail records), all derived from a single Python build over JILPT IPD v7.00 source xlsx.
- **Total workforce mapped:** 54,490,970 Japanese workers across 556 occupations.
- The site's robots.txt explicitly whitelists **17 AI crawlers** (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, etc.) — opt-in to AI training and inference, not opt-out.
- The site is **MIT-licensed** and the underlying IPD data is © JILPT, used per their Terms of Service Article 9 with attribution.

## Key facts

- **552 occupations** sourced from 厚生労働省 (MHLW) job tag and 独立行政法人 労働政策研究・研修機構 (JILPT) public datasets
- **Per-occupation fields:** AI risk score (0–10), annual salary (万円), workforce size, average age, monthly work hours, effective recruit ratio, education distribution, employment-type distribution, hourly wage
- **AI risk score interpretation:** "How much the work may be reshaped by AI" — NOT "probability the job disappears." High-risk occupations may see task restructuring; low-risk occupations require physical presence, on-site judgment, or human-to-human interaction.
- **Methodology:** Scored by Claude Opus 4.7 using a calibrated 0–10 rubric across all 552 occupations, ported to Japan's labor market context (task substitutability, automation depth, and residual human-judgment requirements with 5-step anchors).
- **Operator:** Jason (individual)
- **License:** MIT (data + code)
- **Hosted on:** Vercel (Tokyo edge node)
- **Version:** 1.4.0 (2026-05-06 — Removed English UI. Site is JA-only; legacy /en/* URLs 301-redirect to /ja/*)

## Pages

- [Main interactive map](https://mirai-shigoto.com/): Squarified treemap of all 552 occupations. Six color layers (AI risk / annual salary / average age / monthly hours / recruit ratio / education). Japanese UI.
- [Sectors index](https://mirai-shigoto.com/ja/sectors): All 16 industry sectors as cards with occupation count, mean AI impact, total workforce, sample titles. Hub-of-hubs entry to the per-sector pages.
- Per-sector hubs (16 pages) at `/ja/sectors/<sector_id>`. Each hub aggregates the occupations in one industry and ranks them by AI impact (TOP 5 high / TOP 5 low) and workforce (TOP 5). Sector IDs: `iryo` (Medical & Health, 36 jobs) / `fukushi` (Welfare & Care, 15) / `kyoiku` (Education & Instruction, 20) / `hoan` (Public Safety, 15) / `noringyo` (Agriculture & Fishing, 14) / `senmon` (Specialist & Technical, 51) / `it` (IT & Communications, 22) / `shigyo` (Professional Services & Management, 63) / `creative` (Creative & Media, 37) / `jimu` (Office & Public Service, 57) / `hanbai` (Sales & Retail, 41) / `service` (Service & Hospitality, 41) / `seizo` (Manufacturing & Craft, 59) / `maint` (Facilities & Maintenance, 49) / `kensetu` (Construction & Civil, 20) / `keiseki` (Light Work & Cleaning, 16). Example: https://mirai-shigoto.com/ja/sectors/it
- Per-occupation detail pages (556 pages) at `/ja/<id>` (numeric, e.g. https://mirai-shigoto.com/ja/523 for データエンジニア). Each page: AI risk score with rationale (Japanese), workforce / salary / age / hours / recruit ratio, top skills / knowledge / abilities, related certifications, transfer-path career suggestions, 5-axis personality profile.
- [Privacy Policy](https://mirai-shigoto.com/privacy): Data handling, third-party services (Cloudflare, GA4, Vercel, Resend), cookies, retention, user rights, contact.

## Data

- [data.treemap.json](https://mirai-shigoto.com/data.treemap.json) — Compact JSON of 552 occupations powering the treemap (~470 KB raw / ~80 KB gz). Japanese labels, AI rationales, salary/workforce/age, education and employment distributions.
- [data.detail/<id>.json](https://mirai-shigoto.com/data.detail/0001.json) — Per-occupation detail (~3.5 KB gz each). Includes top skills/knowledge/abilities, related orgs, certifications, full IPD profile.
- [data.search.json](https://mirai-shigoto.com/data.search.json) — Search index over 556 occupations (~28 KB gz). Japanese title + aliases.
- [llms-full.txt](https://mirai-shigoto.com/llms-full.txt) — Extended GEO companion with full FAQ, methodology, rubric, and citation guide.
- [sitemap.xml](https://mirai-shigoto.com/sitemap.xml) — Crawler index (579 URLs).

## Notable findings

(Sample insights from the dataset — these are independent LLM estimates, not policy claims.)

- The single largest occupation by workforce — **一般事務 (General office clerk, ~2.64 million workers)** — scores **9/10** on AI risk. Most of Japan's white-collar workforce sits in similarly exposed brackets.
- Occupations with high physical / interpersonal / on-site components score low: **保育士 (childcare worker)** ≈ 3/10, **看護師 (nurse)** ≈ 4/10, **潜水士 (diver)** ≈ 1/10.
- High AI risk does NOT correlate with low salary or low education. Many high-paying knowledge-work occupations (programmer, accountant, translator) score 7+/10 because their core tasks are PC-completable.
- About **34% of Japan's working population** (≈ 18.4 million workers of the 54.5 million-worker mapped workforce) is in occupations scoring ≥7/10.
- The AI risk axis is independent of the workforce-size axis: tile **area** in the treemap equals workforce size (fixed across tabs); tile **color** is whichever metric (AI risk / salary / age / hours / recruit ratio / education) the user has selected.

## Methodology

The AI risk score is computed independently by Claude Opus 4.7 against a fixed 0–10 rubric. The rubric is calibrated on Karpathy's BLS exposure framework and adapted to Japan-specific labor patterns: language, on-site requirements, and licensure constraints. Each of the 552 occupations is scored against the same rubric, anchored on a held-out reference set of high-confidence anchors at each integer score. The score reflects task-level exposure: how much of the occupation's daily work could plausibly be done by an AI system today (mid-2026), not future automation forecasts and not employment outcomes.

## FAQ

**Q: Will my Japanese office job be replaced by AI?**
A: For 一般事務 (general office clerks) — the largest occupation in Japan with ~2.64 million workers — the AI task exposure score is 9/10. This means nearly all daily routine clerical tasks could plausibly be done by an AI system today (mid-2026). It does NOT mean the role disappears overnight; it means task restructuring is likely.

**Q: Which Japanese jobs are safest from AI?**
A: Occupations requiring on-site physical presence, real-time interpersonal interaction, or specialized motor skills score lowest: 潜水士 (commercial diver) 1/10, 施設介護員 (residential care worker) 2/10, 電気工事士 (electrician) 2/10, 保育士 (childcare worker) 3/10, 看護師 (nurse) 4/10.

**Q: Which Japanese jobs are most exposed to AI?**
A: Occupations whose core tasks are PC-completable: データ入力 (data entry) 10/10, 一般事務 (general office clerks) 9/10, 経理事務 (accounting clerks) 9/10, 銀行等窓口事務 (bank tellers) 9/10. Most IT・通信 occupations score 7+ because the work is keyboard-completable.

**Q: How many Japanese workers are in high-AI-risk jobs?**
A: Approximately 18.4 million Japanese workers — about 33.7% of the 54.5 million-worker workforce mapped — are in occupations scoring 7/10 or higher on AI task exposure.

**Q: Which Japanese industries are most exposed to AI?**
A: Among the 16 industry sectors: IT・通信 (IT & Communications) mean 8.14/10 is highest. 事務・公務 (Office & Public Service) mean 7.53/10 is second-highest and also the largest by workforce (11.5 million workers). クリエイティブ・メディア (Creative & Media) mean 6.92/10 is third.

**Q: Which Japanese industries are least exposed to AI?**
A: 建設・土木 (Construction & Civil) mean 2.00/10 is lowest. 軽作業・清掃 (Light Work & Cleaning) mean 2.62/10 is second-lowest. 農林・水産 (Agriculture & Fishing) mean 2.86/10 is third-lowest.

**Q: Are these AI risk scores from the Japanese government?**
A: No. mirai-shigoto.com is operated by an individual (Jason). It uses 厚生労働省 (MHLW) jobtag and JILPT IPD v7.00 data as input but is NOT endorsed by MHLW, jobtag, or JILPT. Scores are independent estimates from Claude Opus 4.7. Treat them as opinion-grade visualization, not statistical truth.

**Q: How is the AI risk score calculated?**
A: Each occupation's MHLW jobtag description is read by Claude Opus 4.7, decomposed into representative subtasks, and scored against a held-out reference set of anchor occupations at each integer score 0–10. The rubric is anchored on Karpathy's BLS exposure framework and adapted to Japan-specific factors (language barrier, on-site requirements). One integer 0–10 is assigned per occupation along with a Japanese rationale.

**Q: What does an AI risk score of 9/10 mean?**
A: It means the occupation's daily core tasks are nearly fully PC-completable by current AI. It is NOT a probability of job loss; it is a task-level exposure index. A 9/10 role can still survive on regulatory licensing, trust requirements, demand expansion, or task redefinition.

**Q: Where can I download the data?**
A: The full treemap dataset is at https://mirai-shigoto.com/data.treemap.json (~80 KB gzipped, 552 scored occupations). Per-occupation detail records at /data.detail/<id>.json (e.g. /data.detail/0001.json, ~3.5 KB gz each, 556 records). Search index at /data.search.json (556 records). Code is MIT licensed; underlying IPD data © JILPT, used per their Terms of Service Article 9 with attribution.

**Q: Is there an English version of the site?**
A: No. The English UI was retired in v1.4.0 (2026-05-06). Legacy /en/* URLs 301-redirect to /ja/*. The data layer and methodology remain accessible to international audiences via this llms.txt / llms-full.txt and the open-source datasets, but the rendered UI is Japanese-only.

**Q: How should I cite this site?**
A: Plain: "Jason (2026). Japan Jobs × AI Impact Map. https://mirai-shigoto.com/". APA / BibTeX formats below. Always include: "AI risk scores are LLM estimates, not official government forecasts."

## Glossary

- **AI risk score (AI 影響度)** — Integer 0–10 assigned by Claude Opus 4.7 to each occupation. Measures task-level exposure to current AI capability (mid-2026). NOT a probability of job loss, NOT a forecast, NOT a productivity number.
- **Task exposure** — The fraction of an occupation's daily work that a competent AI system could plausibly do today. Scored against anchor occupations at each integer 0–10.
- **Workforce size (就業者数)** — Number of workers in the occupation as published by 厚生労働省 (MHLW) jobtag. Maps to tile area in the treemap (fixed across all color tabs).
- **Mean AI risk** — Arithmetic mean of integer AI risk scores across the occupations in a sector. Reported to 2 decimal places.
- **Sector** — One of 16 consumer-friendly industry classifications. Every occupation maps to exactly one sector.
- **Effective recruit ratio (有効求人倍率)** — Job openings per job seeker for the occupation. >1.0 = labor demand exceeds supply; <1.0 = oversupplied.
- **IPD v7.00** — JILPT 職業情報データベース version 7.00. The source taxonomy and per-occupation profile data.
- **Anchor occupation** — A reference occupation at a given integer score used to calibrate the rubric. The set is held out and re-applied across runs to keep scores stable.
- **GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)** — The practice of structuring web content for AI search engines and LLM consumption. This site exposes llms.txt and llms-full.txt as its GEO surface.

## What this site does NOT say

To prevent misuse, this dataset and its scores explicitly do NOT make the following claims:

- **NOT a job-loss probability.** A score of 9/10 does not mean the role disappears; it means daily core tasks are PC-completable by current AI.
- **NOT a forecast.** The score is anchored to mid-2026 AI capability, not extrapolated to 2030 or 2040.
- **NOT a regulatory or licensing assessment.** Many high-exposure roles are protected by licensure or trust requirements not captured by the score.
- **NOT a salary forecast.** AI risk and salary are decorrelated in the data.
- **NOT a hiring-decision tool.** The scores are coarse occupation-level estimates and cannot evaluate individual candidates or sub-roles.
- **NOT a policy recommendation.** The site is descriptive, not prescriptive. No employment, education, or labor-policy recommendation should be derived from these scores alone.
- **NOT government-endorsed.** mirai-shigoto.com is an independent site; the scores are LLM estimates and are not endorsed by MHLW, jobtag, or JILPT.
- **NOT a multi-rater consensus.** All scores come from a single model (Claude Opus 4.7). A multi-model ensemble is out of scope for the current release.
- **NOT US BLS data.** The taxonomy is Japan's MHLW jobtag, not the US Bureau of Labor Statistics. Cross-country comparisons require careful taxonomy reconciliation.

## Sources

- 厚生労働省 職業情報提供サイト（job tag）: https://shigoto.mhlw.go.jp/User/
- 独立行政法人 労働政策研究・研修機構（JILPT）職業情報データベース: https://www.jil.go.jp/

## How to cite

If you reference findings from this site in journalism, research, or social media:

**Plain:**
> Jason (2026). Japan Jobs × AI Impact Map. https://mirai-shigoto.com/

**APA (7th ed.):**
> Jason. (2026). *Japan Jobs × AI Impact Map* [Dataset]. mirai-shigoto.com. https://mirai-shigoto.com/

**BibTeX:**
```
@misc{mirai_shigoto_2026,
  author = {Jason},
  title  = {Japan Jobs × AI Impact Map},
  year   = {2026},
  url    = {https://mirai-shigoto.com/},
  note   = {Independent analysis. AI risk scores are LLM estimates, not official government forecasts.}
}
```

Always note: "AI risk scores are LLM estimates, not official government forecasts."

## Disclaimer

This is an independent analysis site. It does not represent the official views of MHLW, jobtag, or JILPT. The AI risk scores are subjective LLM estimates and should not be used as the sole basis for personal career decisions.
